options.label: Stops when MASTER SWITCH released from START

ge-q2.text: Is the DEAD CRANK switch set to NORMAL?

options.label: No — it was not set to NORMAL

ge-a1.text: Set DEAD CRANK switch to NORMAL
ge-a1.actionText: Set the DEAD CRANK switch to the NORMAL position. Try cranking the engine again.

ge-q3.text: Is the DC CONTROL POWER circuit breaker pushed in (ON)?

ge-a2.text: Reset DC CONTROL POWER circuit breaker
ge-a2.actionText: Push the DC CONTROL POWER circuit breaker back in to the ON position. Try cranking the engine again.

ge-q4.text: Is the Emergency Stop Switch pulled out (released)?

ge-a3.actionText: Pull the Emergency Stop Switch out to release it. Try cranking the engine again.

ge-q5.text: Check battery connections. Are they clean, tight, and free of corrosion?

ge-a4.actionText: Disconnect negative cable first, then positive. Clean terminals and cable clamps with a wire brush. Reconnect positive first, then negative. Ensure connections are tight. Try cranking again.

ge-q6.text: Is the ambient temperature below 40 degrees F?

ge-a5.actionText: Use the PREHEAT position on the MASTER SWITCH to preheat the engine before cranking. Hold PREHEAT for the time specified on the data plate (usually 30-60 seconds), then move to START.

ge-q6a.text: Does the engine start now after preheating?

ge-q7.text: Check the air cleaner restriction indicator. Is the air cleaner dirty or clogged?

ge-a6.actionText: Remove and clean or replace the air cleaner element. Reset the restriction indicator. Try starting the engine again.

ge-q8.text: Is the fuel filter/water separator dirty or full of water?

ge-a7.actionText: Drain the water from the fuel filter/water separator. If the filter element is dirty, replace it. Prime the fuel system after servicing. Try starting the engine again.

ge-q10.text: Did you hold the MASTER SWITCH in START until oil pressure reached at least 25 psi?

options.label: No — I released it too soon
Yes — I held it until 25 psi

ge-a8.text: Hold MASTER SWITCH in START longer
ge-a8.actionText: Turn the MASTER SWITCH to START and hold it there. Watch the oil pressure gauge. Do not release until oil pressure reaches at least 25 psi. The engine will shut down if released before oil pressure is sufficient.

ge-q10a.text: Does the engine stay running now?

ge-q12.text: Check the FAULT INDICATOR lights on the control panel. Are any fault lights on?

ge-q13.text: Is the DC CONTROL POWER circuit breaker tripped (popped out)?

ge-a9.text: Reset DC CONTROL POWER circuit breaker
ge-a9.actionText: Push the DC CONTROL POWER circuit breaker back in. Restart the generator and observe. If it trips again immediately, do not reset again.

ge-q13a.text: Does the generator stay running now?

options.label: No — trips again or still stops

ge-q14.text: Check the air cleaner restriction indicator. Is the air cleaner dirty or clogged?

ge-a10.actionText: Remove and clean or replace the air cleaner element. Reset the restriction indicator. Restart and check if the engine runs smoothly.

ge-q14a.text: Does the engine run smoothly now?

ge-q15.text: Could the fuel be contaminated (water, dirt, or wrong fuel type)?

ge-a11.actionText: Drain the fuel filter/water separator. If water or debris is present, replace the filter element and prime the fuel system. Try running the generator again.

ge-q15a.text: Does the engine run smoothly now?

ge-q16.text: Is the generator using the correct fuel type (DF-2 diesel or JP-8)?

ge-q18.text: Check the air cleaner restriction indicator. Is the air cleaner dirty or clogged?

ge-a12.actionText: Remove and clean or replace the air cleaner element. Reset the restriction indicator. Run the generator and check if it develops full power.

ge-q18a.text: Does the generator develop full power now?

ge-a13.actionText: Drain the fuel filter/water separator. If water or debris is present, replace the filter element and prime the fuel system.

ge-q19a.text: Does the generator develop full power now?

ge-q20.text: Is the exhaust system restricted or damaged?

ge-q21.text: Is the generator using the correct fuel type?

ge-q22.text: Check the oil level on the dipstick. Is the oil low?

ge-a14.actionText: Add the correct type and weight of engine oil until the dipstick reads in the safe operating range. Run the engine and listen for the knock.

ge-q22a.text: Is the knocking gone after adding oil?

ge-q23.text: Can you identify any loose parts or foreign objects inside the generator housing?

options.label: Yes — found loose parts or foreign objects

ge-a15.text: Remove foreign objects and secure loose parts
ge-a15.actionText: Shut down the generator. Remove any foreign objects. Tighten any loose hardware. Restart and listen for the knock.

ge-q24.text: Is the generator using the correct fuel type?

ge-t1.text: Engine cranks after correcting switch position.

terminal.summary: DEAD CRANK switch was not in NORMAL — corrected

ge-t2.text: Engine fails to crank after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Engine fails to crank — beyond operator repair

ge-t3.text: Battery connections cleaned and tightened. Engine cranks.

terminal.summary: Battery connections corroded/loose — cleaned and tightened

ge-t4.text: Cold weather start — preheating resolved the issue.

terminal.summary: Cold weather — used PREHEAT, engine started

ge-t5.text: Air cleaner was dirty. Serviced and engine runs properly.

terminal.summary: Dirty air cleaner — serviced, engine runs properly

ge-t6.text: Engine cranks but will not start after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Engine cranks but won't start — beyond operator repair

ge-t7.text: Fuel filter/water separator serviced. Engine runs properly.

terminal.summary: Fuel filter/water separator dirty — serviced, engine runs

ge-t8.text: Engine stops when MASTER SWITCH is released from START. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Engine stops on switch release — beyond operator repair

ge-t9.text: Engine stays running. Operator was releasing MASTER SWITCH too early.

terminal.summary: Improper starting procedure — held START until 25 psi

ge-t10.text: Fault indicator lights are on. The generator has detected a fault condition. Shut down and notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Fault indicator lights on — generator shutdown, needs maintenance

ge-t11.text: Engine stops suddenly with no clear operator-level cause. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Engine stops suddenly — beyond operator repair

ge-t12.text: DC CONTROL POWER circuit breaker was tripped. Reset and generator runs.

terminal.summary: DC CONTROL POWER breaker tripped — reset, generator runs

ge-t13.text: Wrong fuel type in the generator. Notify next higher maintenance level to drain and refuel with correct fuel.

terminal.summary: Improper fuel type — needs drain and refuel

ge-t14.text: Engine runs erratically after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Engine runs erratically — beyond operator repair

ge-t15.text: Exhaust system appears restricted or damaged. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Restricted/damaged exhaust — needs maintenance

ge-t16.text: Generator does not develop full power after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Low power output — beyond operator repair

ge-t17.text: Oil was low causing engine knock. Oil added, knock resolved.

terminal.summary: Low oil caused knock — oil added, knock resolved

ge-t18.text: Loose parts or foreign objects removed. Knock resolved.

terminal.summary: Foreign objects/loose parts removed — knock resolved

ge-t19.text: Engine knocks after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Engine knock — beyond operator repair

gx-q1.text: What color is the exhaust smoke?

gx-q2.text: Is the generator using the correct fuel type (DF-2 diesel or JP-8)?

gx-q3.text: Is the generator using the correct fuel type?

gx-q4.text: Check the air cleaner restriction indicator. Is the air cleaner dirty or clogged?

gx-a1.actionText: Remove and clean or replace the air cleaner element. Reset the restriction indicator. Run the generator and observe the exhaust.

gx-t1.text: Wrong fuel type in the generator. Notify next higher maintenance level to drain and refuel.

terminal.summary: Improper fuel type causing exhaust smoke — needs drain and refuel

gx-t2.text: Blue or white exhaust smoke with correct fuel. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Blue/white exhaust smoke — beyond operator repair

gx-t3.text: Black exhaust smoke after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Black exhaust smoke — beyond operator repair

gx-t4.text: Air cleaner was dirty causing black smoke. Serviced and exhaust is clear.

terminal.summary: Dirty air cleaner caused black smoke — serviced, exhaust clear

go-q1.text: Oil pressure is low. Check the oil level on the dipstick. Is the oil low?

go-a1.actionText: Add the correct type and weight of engine oil until the dipstick reads in the safe operating range. Run the engine and check oil pressure.

go-q3.text: Check the coolant level. Is it low?

go-a2.actionText: Allow the engine to cool. Remove the radiator cap carefully. Add coolant to the proper level. Replace the cap and run the engine. Check oil pressure.

go-q4.text: Is the air intake or exhaust obstructed with debris?

go-a3.actionText: Shut down the generator. Clear all debris from the air intake grills, exhaust grills, and surrounding area. Restart and check oil pressure.

go-q5.text: Is the fan belt loose or slipping?

go-t1.text: Oil was low causing low pressure. Oil added, pressure is normal.

terminal.summary: Low oil level — added oil, pressure normal

go-t2.text: Engine was overheating due to low coolant, which caused low oil pressure. Coolant added.

terminal.summary: Low coolant caused overheating/low oil pressure — coolant added

go-t3.text: Air intake was obstructed causing overheating and low oil pressure. Debris removed.

terminal.summary: Air intake obstructed — debris removed, pressure normal

go-t4.text: Fan belt is loose, which may cause overheating and low oil pressure. Notify next higher maintenance level for belt adjustment.

terminal.summary: Loose fan belt — needs maintenance for adjustment

go-t5.text: Low oil pressure after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Low oil pressure — beyond operator repair

gc-q1.text: Coolant temperature indicator shows overheating. Check the ammeter PERCENT RATED CURRENT. Is the generator overloaded (above 100%)?

gc-a1.actionText: Disconnect or turn off enough electrical loads to bring the ammeter PERCENT RATED CURRENT below 100%. Allow the generator to cool down and check the temperature indicator.

gc-q1a.text: Does the temperature come down after reducing load?

gc-q2.text: Check the coolant level. Is it low?

gc-a2.actionText: Shut down the generator and allow it to cool. Remove the radiator cap carefully. Add coolant to the proper level. Replace the cap and restart. Monitor the temperature.

gc-q3.text: Check the oil level on the dipstick. Is it low?

gc-a3.actionText: Add the correct type and weight of engine oil until the dipstick reads in the safe operating range. Run the engine and monitor the temperature.

gc-q3a.text: Is the temperature dropping to normal?

gc-q4.text: Is the air intake or exhaust obstructed with debris?

gc-a4.actionText: Shut down the generator. Clear all debris from the air intake grills, exhaust grills, and surrounding area. Restart and monitor the temperature.

gc-q4a.text: Is the temperature dropping to normal?

gc-q5.text: Is the fan belt loose or slipping?

gc-t1.text: Generator was overloaded. Load reduced, temperature returning to normal.

terminal.summary: Generator overloaded — load reduced, temp normal

gc-t2.text: Generator still overheating after reducing load. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Overheating despite load reduction — needs maintenance

gc-t3.text: Coolant was low causing overheating. Coolant added, temperature normal.

terminal.summary: Low coolant — added coolant, temp normal

gc-t4.text: Oil was low contributing to overheating. Oil added, temperature normal.

terminal.summary: Low oil — added oil, temp normal

gc-t5.text: Air intake was obstructed causing overheating. Debris removed, temperature normal.

terminal.summary: Air intake obstructed — debris removed, temp normal

gc-t6.text: Fan belt is loose causing insufficient cooling. Notify next higher maintenance level for belt adjustment.

terminal.summary: Loose fan belt — needs maintenance for adjustment

gc-t7.text: Generator overheating after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Overheating — beyond operator repair

gl-q1.text: What electrical problem are you experiencing?

options.label: Battery not charging or low charge

gl-q2.text: Check the BATTERY CHARGER FUSE. Is it blown?

gl-q3.text: Is the fan belt intact and tight? A loose or broken belt prevents the alternator from charging.

gl-q4.text: Check for loose or broken wires at the battery and alternator. Any problems?

options.label: Yes — loose or broken wires found

gl-q6.text: Check the battery electrolyte level. Is it low?

gl-q7.text: Are battery connections clean and tight?

options.label: No — connections are corroded or loose

gl-a1.actionText: Disconnect negative cable first, then positive. Clean terminals and cable clamps with a wire brush. Reconnect positive first, then negative. Ensure connections are tight.

gl-q8.text: Try adjusting the VOLTAGE ADJUST potentiometer. Turn it slightly clockwise to increase voltage.

options.label: Voltage came up to normal range
Voltage did not change or still low

gl-q9.text: Check for loose or broken wires at the output box and generator. Any problems?

options.label: Yes — loose or broken wires found

gl-q10.text: Press the AC CIRCUIT INTERRUPTER test button. Does it trip and reset properly?

options.label: No — does not trip or reset properly

gl-q11.text: Check the load cable connections at the output box. Are they tight and in good condition?

options.label: No — connections are loose or damaged

gl-a2.actionText: Shut down the generator. Tighten all load cable connections at the output box. Inspect cables for damage. Restart and apply load.

gl-q11a.text: Does the AC circuit interrupter still trip?

gl-q12.text: Does the total load exceed the generator's rated capacity? Check the ammeter and compare to the rating plate.

gl-a3.actionText: Disconnect or turn off enough electrical loads to bring the total below the generator's rated capacity. Reset the AC circuit interrupter and apply load gradually.

gl-q12a.text: Does the AC circuit interrupter hold now?

gl-q14.text: Check the Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter (GFCI) at the convenience receptacle. Is it tripped?

gl-a4.actionText: Press the RESET button on the Ground Fault Circuit Interrupter. Check for voltage at the convenience receptacle.

gl-q14a.text: Is there voltage at the convenience receptacle now?

gl-q15.text: Check the convenience receptacle fuse. Is it blown?

gl-t1.text: BATTERY CHARGER FUSE is blown. Notify next higher maintenance level for fuse replacement.

terminal.summary: Battery charger fuse blown — needs replacement

gl-t2.text: Fan belt is loose or broken, preventing alternator from charging. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Loose/broken fan belt — battery not charging, needs maintenance

gl-t3.text: Loose or broken wires found. Notify next higher maintenance level for repair.

terminal.summary: Loose/broken wiring — needs maintenance

gl-t4.text: Battery not charging after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Battery not charging — beyond operator repair

gl-t5.text: Battery electrolyte is low, which can cause excessive charging. Notify next higher maintenance level — do not add water to sealed batteries.

terminal.summary: Low battery electrolyte — needs maintenance

gl-t6.text: Excessive battery charging after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Excessive battery charging — beyond operator repair

gl-t7.text: Battery connections were corroded or loose. Cleaned and tightened, charging is normal.

terminal.summary: Battery connections cleaned — charging normal

gl-t8.text: Voltage adjusted using the VOLTAGE ADJUST potentiometer. Output is now in normal range.

terminal.summary: Voltage adjust potentiometer set — voltage normal

gl-t9.text: AC voltage is low after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: Low AC voltage — beyond operator repair

gl-t10.text: AC circuit interrupter does not function properly. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: AC circuit interrupter malfunction — needs maintenance

gl-t11.text: Load cable connections were loose. Tightened, generator running normally.

terminal.summary: Loose load cable connections — tightened, running normally

gl-t12.text: AC circuit interrupter continues to trip after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: AC circuit interrupter tripping — beyond operator repair

gl-t13.text: Generator was overloaded causing the interrupter to trip. Load reduced, running normally.

terminal.summary: Generator overloaded — load reduced, interrupter holds

gl-t14.text: GFCI was tripped. Reset and convenience receptacle has voltage.

terminal.summary: GFCI tripped — reset, receptacle has voltage

gl-t15.text: Convenience receptacle fuse is blown. Notify next higher maintenance level for fuse replacement.

terminal.summary: Convenience receptacle fuse blown — needs replacement

gl-t16.text: No voltage at convenience receptacle after all operator checks. Notify next higher maintenance level.

terminal.summary: No voltage at receptacle — beyond operator repair
